techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use repeated login attempts, password spraying, or credential stuffing to gain access to accounts.
techniques.detection: Monitor authentication logs for excessive failures from the same source, failures across many accounts, and success immediately after repeated failures.
techniques.mitigation: Enforce multifactor authentication, account lockout policies, strong password controls, and adaptive throttling.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and scripting interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or payloads.
techniques.detection: Monitor shell, PowerShell, cmd.exe, Python, and bash execution with suspicious arguments or encoded commands.
techniques.mitigation: Restrict script execution, enforce application control, log command-line arguments, and disable unused interpreters.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse valid credentials to access systems and evade detection.
techniques.detection: Monitor unusual successful logins, impossible travel, new source locations, and success following many failed attempts.
techniques.mitigation: Use multifactor authentication, credential rotation, least privilege, and user behavior analytics.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use valid credentials or exploitation to move laterally through remote services such as SMB, RDP, SSH, or WinRM.
techniques.detection: Monitor internal-to-internal remote service connections, workstation-to-workstation SMB/RDP, and unusual admin logons.
techniques.mitigation: Segment networks, restrict remote services, enforce privileged access management, and monitor administrative protocols.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit internet-facing applications to gain initial access.
techniques.detection: Monitor web logs for exploit payloads, abnormal HTTP errors, web shell creation, and application process child execution.
techniques.mitigation: Patch exposed applications, use WAF rules, minimize internet exposure, and run vulnerability scanning.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to trick users into revealing credentials or executing payloads.
techniques.detection: Monitor email security events, suspicious links, attachment execution, and credential submissions after email delivery.
techniques.mitigation: Use email filtering, user training, attachment sandboxing, and phishing-resistant authentication.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries may encrypt data to disrupt availability and extort victims.
techniques.detection: Monitor mass file rename/write activity, encryption extensions, shadow copy deletion, and backup service termination.
techniques.mitigation: Maintain offline backups, restrict write permissions, enable behavior-based ransomware detection, and segment critical storage.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal data by sending it over an existing command-and-control channel.
techniques.detection: Monitor unusual outbound traffic volumes, long-lived sessions, and data transfers to suspicious destinations.
techniques.mitigation: Inspect egress traffic, restrict outbound destinations, and alert on anomalous transfer patterns.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using common application layer protocols such as HTTP, DNS, or mail protocols.
techniques.detection: Monitor unusual protocol usage, suspicious beaconing cadence, and encoded payloads in protocol fields.
techniques.mitigation: Use proxy inspection, DNS filtering, allowlists, and egress monitoring.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Adversaries may inject code into processes to evade defenses or gain privileges.
techniques.detection: Monitor suspicious cross-process memory access, unusual child processes, and endpoint injection alerts.
techniques.mitigation: Enable exploit protection, restrict debug privileges, and use endpoint detection controls.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Adversaries may dump credentials from operating system stores, memory, or registry locations.
techniques.detection: Monitor LSASS access, credential dumping tool signatures, and suspicious reads of credential stores.
techniques.mitigation: Enable credential protection, limit privileged access, and block known dumping tools.

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may create local, domain, or cloud accounts to maintain access.
techniques.detection: Monitor account creation events, unexpected privileged group membership, and new service accounts.
techniques.mitigation: Restrict account creation permissions, alert on privileged account changes, and review dormant accounts.

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Adversaries may bypass or abuse privilege controls such as sudo, UAC, or SUID binaries.
techniques.detection: Monitor sudo misuse, UAC bypass patterns, SUID execution, and privilege changes by non-admin users.
techniques.mitigation: Apply least privilege, restrict sudo rules, patch local privilege escalation bugs, and harden UAC policies.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries may create scheduled tasks or jobs to execute malicious code persistently.
techniques.detection: Monitor new scheduled tasks, cron changes, unusual run times, and tasks executing from temporary directories.
techniques.mitigation: Restrict task creation, review scheduled jobs, and alert on suspicious task actions.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may disable or modify security tools, logging, or recovery services.
techniques.detection: Monitor service stop events, tamper protection alerts, logging configuration changes, and security tool exclusion updates.
techniques.mitigation: Enable tamper protection, restrict administrative rights, and centralize immutable logging.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or modify logs, files, or artifacts to hide activity.
techniques.detection: Monitor log clearing events, suspicious file deletion, timestamp tampering, and audit policy changes.
techniques.mitigation: Forward logs off-host, restrict log deletion permissions, and enable file integrity monitoring.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or payloads into a compromised environment.
techniques.detection: Monitor curl, wget, PowerShell downloads, unusual outbound requests, and newly created executables.
techniques.mitigation: Restrict outbound access, inspect downloads, and block untrusted execution paths.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories to identify valuable data.
techniques.detection: Monitor recursive directory listings, access to sensitive shares, and unusual file browsing patterns.
techniques.mitigation: Limit file permissions, hide sensitive shares, and monitor access to high-value directories.

techniques.name: System Information Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may collect system information such as OS version, hostname, users, or hardware details.
techniques.detection: Monitor commands such as systeminfo, uname, hostname, whoami, and environment enumeration.
techniques.mitigation: Limit command execution, reduce exposed system metadata, and alert on unusual discovery chains.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate remote hosts and domain systems to plan lateral movement.
techniques.detection: Monitor net view, ping sweeps, LDAP queries, ARP scans, and internal service enumeration.
techniques.mitigation: Segment networks, restrict directory queries, and alert on discovery bursts.

techniques.name: Account Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate accounts to identify targets for credential access or privilege escalation.
techniques.detection: Monitor net user, LDAP account enumeration, cloud identity listing, and group membership queries.
techniques.mitigation: Restrict directory enumeration, monitor identity provider APIs, and apply least privilege.

techniques.name: Network Service Scanning
techniques.description: Adversaries may scan hosts for open ports and services to identify attack paths.
techniques.detection: Monitor connection attempts across many ports or hosts, nmap signatures, and rapid service enumeration.
techniques.mitigation: Segment networks, restrict unnecessary services, and deploy network intrusion detection.

techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
techniques.description: Adversaries may tunnel one protocol through another to evade network controls.
techniques.detection: Monitor long-lived sessions, unusual protocol nesting, and atypical traffic volume for approved protocols.
techniques.mitigation: Inspect egress traffic, restrict tunneling tools, and enforce protocol-aware filtering.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over protocols other than the main command-and-control channel, such as DNS or FTP.
techniques.detection: Monitor DNS queries with large payloads, uncommon outbound protocols, and repeated small transfers to one destination.
techniques.mitigation: Limit allowed egress protocols, inspect DNS, and alert on abnormal transfer patterns.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data to cloud storage or web services that blend with normal traffic.
techniques.detection: Monitor unusual uploads to web services, new OAuth app grants, and large transfers to unsanctioned destinations.
techniques.mitigation: Use data loss prevention, restrict unsanctioned cloud storage, and monitor cloud application access.
